overview.description: Elmwood Park is a diverse, working-class borough in southern Bergen County bordered by the Passaic River to the south and Saddle River to the west. Originally part of Saddle River Township, it became an independent borough in 1916. Elmwood Park offers affordable housing by Bergen County standards, convenient access to major highways, and a tight-knit community with strong ethnic heritage — particularly Italian-American and Polish-American traditions.
highlights: Affordable housing compared to much of Bergen County
Strong Italian-American and Polish-American cultural heritage
Convenient access to Routes 4, 17, 46, and I-80
Bordered by the scenic Passaic and Saddle Rivers
Active recreation programs and community events

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Elmwood Park's housing stock is predominantly single-family homes and two-family homes built in the mid-20th century.
realEstate.recentTrends: Elmwood Park has seen steady price appreciation as buyers seek more affordable alternatives to pricier Bergen County towns. The market is strong for starter homes and two-family investment properties. Renovation activity is increasing as older housing stock is updated.
neighborhoodNotes: The Market Street corridor serves as the main commercial artery with shops, restaurants, and services
The area near Memorial School features well-kept single-family homes on tree-lined streets
The Passaic River area in the south has larger lots but some flood risk
The Route 4 corridor on the northern border has more commercial development and multi-family housing

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the borough website
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally in biodegradable bags

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Passaic Valley Water Commission and sewer service is provided by the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Passaic Valley Water Commission
Sewer charges included in property tax bills
Water quality reports available annually from PVWC
Report water main breaks to the DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Elmwood Park Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural work, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC modifications
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Applications submitted at Borough Hall on Market Street

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Elmwood Park manages street parking and municipal lots throughout the borough.
details: Free street parking available on most residential streets
Municipal parking near the Market Street commercial area
Overnight parking restrictions during winter snow emergencies
No parking within 25 feet of intersections

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Elmwood Park Senior Center offers programs, transportation, and social activities for residents aged 60+.
details: Located at the Recreation Center on Market Street
Transportation to medical appointments and shopping
Daily activities including cards, bingo, and exercise classes
Annual trips and seasonal celebrations

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department runs youth sports, summer camps, and community events throughout the year.
details: Youth basketball, soccer, baseball, and football leagues
Summer recreation program for school-age children
Adult fitness and recreational programs
Community pool at Boro Field during summer months

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Elmwood Park enforces noise restrictions to maintain residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Excessive noise from residential properties (music, parties) subject to fines
Leaf blower use restricted to certain hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards including landscaping and structural upkeep.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed — grass over 10 inches may result in violation
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles on properties
Building facades must be maintained in safe condition
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in height in rear yards, 4 feet in front yards

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and kept under control at all times.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets on public and private property
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Maximum number of pets may be limited by ordinance

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Elmwood Park enforces parking regulations on residential and commercial streets.
keyRules: No parking during snow emergencies on designated streets
Alternate-side parking enforced for street sweeping in some areas
Commercial vehicles over certain weight limits prohibited from parking on residential streets
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required at all times
No parking on lawns or unpaved surfaces

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Elmwood Park regulates rental properties to ensure safety and habitability standards.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental units with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required for all rental units
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family dwellings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Elmwood Park Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted but regulated by borough ordinance.
keyRules: Permit required from the Borough Clerk's office
Limited to a certain number of sales per year per property
Signs must be removed after the sale concludes
Sales restricted to personal household items

publicSchools.name: Elmwood Park Memorial High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school offering AP courses, vocational programs, and a strong athletics program

publicSchools.name: Elmwood Park Memorial Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Serves all borough middle school students with honors and enrichment programs

privateSchools.name: St. Leo School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with St. Leo's Parish, offering faith-based education

whyMoveHere: More affordable housing compared to many Bergen County communities
Convenient highway access to Routes 4, 17, 46, and I-80
Strong sense of community with active civic organizations
Diverse population with rich Italian-American and Polish-American heritage
Close proximity to Paramus shopping and entertainment

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes by bus; 30-60 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus service runs to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. Car commute via Route 4 to the George Washington Bridge or via I-80 to the Lincoln Tunnel. Nearby Saddle Brook and Fair Lawn offer additional bus routes.

costOfLiving.summary: Elmwood Park offers a more affordable cost of living compared to northern Bergen County, making it attractive for first-time buyers and families.

pros: Affordable home prices relative to Bergen County
Strong community feel with active local organizations
Good local dining scene with authentic Italian and Polish restaurants
Borough pool and recreation programs for families
cons: Highway noise near Routes 4 and 46 corridors
Some areas prone to flooding near the Passaic River
School ratings below Bergen County's top districts
Limited nightlife and upscale dining within the borough
Aging housing stock requiring renovation in some areas

restaurants.name: EJ's Luncheonette
restaurants.description: Classic family-owned diner serving generous portions of traditional American breakfast and lunch fare in a nostalgic setting.

restaurants.name: Taste of Poland
restaurants.description: Authentic Polish restaurant offering pierogis, kielbasa, stuffed cabbage, and other traditional Eastern European dishes.

restaurants.name: La Pergola
restaurants.description: Popular Italian restaurant serving classic red-sauce dishes, fresh pasta, and wood-fired pizza in a warm family setting.

restaurants.name: Cubby's BBQ
restaurants.description: Local barbecue joint known for smoked brisket, ribs, pulled pork, and hearty sides with a loyal following.

parks.name: Boro Field
parks.description: Elmwood Park's main recreation complex featuring baseball fields, a community pool, basketball courts, and a playground.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park — Elmwood Park Section
parks.description: A section of the linear Bergen County park along the Saddle River, offering walking trails, picnic areas, and nature viewing.
parks.address: Saddle River Road, Elmwood Park, NJ 07407

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Quiet memorial park honoring local veterans with monuments, benches, and green space for reflection.

events.name: Elmwood Park Day
events.description: Annual borough-wide celebration featuring live music, food vendors, carnival rides, and community entertainment.

events.name: Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Fourth of July festivities with a parade, family activities at Boro Field, and evening fireworks.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony at Borough Hall with holiday music, refreshments, and a visit from Santa.

attractions.name: Elmwood Park Public Library
attractions.description: Community library offering an extensive collection, children's programs, computer access, and cultural events throughout the year.

attractions.name: Saddle River Greenway
attractions.description: Multi-use path along the Saddle River suitable for walking, jogging, and cycling, connecting to adjacent towns' trail systems.
attractions.address: Along the Saddle River, Elmwood Park, NJ

shoppingAreas: Market Street — Main commercial strip with shops, restaurants, bakeries, and local businesses
Broadway — Additional retail and services along the major road corridor
Route 4 Corridor — Big-box retail and chain restaurants near the Paramus border

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Elmwood Park Borough Hall, 182 Market Street, Elmwood Park, NJ 07407

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
